steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
Sum from the frequency table:
$$13(12)+14(8)+15(5)+16(7)+17(9)+18(10)+19(13)+20(7)=1175.$$ The original mean is $\frac{1175}{71}\approx16.55$. After adding $39$, the new mean is $\frac{1214}{72}\approx16.86$, so the mean increases.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content:
Original (71 values): The median is at position $\frac{71+1}{2}=36$. Track the cumulative totals:
  • Weight 13: cumulative total 12
  • Weight 14: cumulative total 20
  • Weight 15: cumulative total 25
  • Weight 16: cumulative total 32
  • Weight 17: cumulative total 41
Position 36 first appears in the weight-17 group, so the original median is 17.

New (72 values): The added value 39 is the largest, so it occupies position 72. Positions 36 and 37 still hold weight 17, giving a new median of $\frac{17+17}{2}=17$.

The medians are equal.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: The new mean is greater, and the medians are equal. That matches Choice B — The mean of the new data set is greater than the mean of the original data set, and the medians of the two data sets are equal..
